parsed_review.summary: CP00-188 is a Risk A, descriptor-only Matter Core permission/audit security fixture boundary pack (RP05.P06.M05.S16-RP05.P06.M06.S03, 10 units). Read-only inspection of the seven in-scope implementation refs confirms the production_ready_flag boundary (matter_core_permission_audit_security_fixture_boundary_verified) is genuinely supported. registry.js/service.js/validators.js build only frozen synthetic descriptor rows; a grep for writeFileSync/fetch/http/exec/fs/document/eval/process.env found no real side effects in-scope. NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and leak-guard flags hold (no runtime permission/audit evaluation, no audit write, no route dispatch, no live DOM, no API execution, no permission-decision/audit-body/unauthorized-count leak, no Loop engine), stable IDs and replay commands are inert, and unauthorized_count is null. The contract projection, focused node:test suite, and rp05:matter-core:validate assert the same boundaries (allowed->passed, denied/cross-tenant->blocked, leak-prevention binds security-trimming proof, customer-safe error codes only) and coverage matches baseline (4 security_audit / 4 test / 2 implementation; M05=7, M06=3). Claude is preserved as a read-only reviewer (allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob, claude_is_final_approval=false, no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation), and Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime. CP178+ hardening forbidden-evidence list is encoded in-contract; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON and there are no pre-existing raw-output/review-receipt artifacts presenting invalid evidence as valid. Unrelated dirty files (apps/web, amplitude/progress-control-room scripts) are correctly scoped out and not treated as pack content. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One P3 (expected, non-blocking): the documented downstream gates (full validation suite, adjudication, construction inspection, and exactly one valid hardened review receipt) must complete before production_ready flips; the manifest correctly keeps production_ready=false/in_progress. Enterprise trust closure remains gated on human final approval, not this review.

findings.title: Final closeout validation suite and downstream gates remain pending (expected, non-blocking)
findings.details: command-evidence.json records node --check syntax validations, the focused `node --test packages/matter/test/model.test.js`, and `npm run rp05:matter-core:validate` as passed (exit 0), with gate_outcome=pending_hardened_review_and_final_validation. The broader expected suite (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is not yet recorded as executed. Correspondingly, claude-review-result.json shows exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false with empty valid_review_receipts, adjudication.md is 'pending', and construction-inspection.json verdict is PENDING. This is consistent with the documented hardened_review_sequence (baseline -> readiness -> read_only_claude_raw_capture -> normalize -> receipt_validate -> adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation), where final validation follows this read-only review. Command evidence is self-reported; as a read-only reviewer I verified source and artifacts, not live command execution.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping production_ready to true and committing, run the full validation suite, complete adjudication and construction inspection, and capture exactly one valid hardened review receipt (rejecting auth failures, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing required fields). Human final approval remains required for any enterprise trust claim; this review does not constitute final approval.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected next step in the hardened review sequence; non-blocking. Manifest honestly reflects production_ready=false/in_progress, so no remediation to source is required.
